mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month payroll growth above 100,000 persons signals robust labor demand and consumer income expansion, which drives equity valuations higher through increased corporate earnings expectations over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2010 payroll level at 159,005 thousand persons
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with positive employment momentum
failure_condition: Payroll growth exceeds 100k persons in month but S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of such instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate above 4.5% combined with month-over-month CPI acceleration signals monetary tightening that raises real discount rates, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation pressure
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS remains above 4.5% with CPI acceleration but 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered periods, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts measured by HOUST decline more than 10% month-over-month, signaling weakening residential construction demand and forward-looking consumer confidence deterioration, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprrice growth expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,354 thousand units in March 2010
failure_condition: HOUST declines more than 10% month-over-month but S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of triggered instances, or signal shows Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index INDPRO rises month-over-month by more than 0.5% while unemployment rate UNRATE remains stable or declines, indicating genuine supply-side capacity expansion and productivity gains that support equity earnings growth over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change > 0.5% AND UNRATE month-over-month change <= 0%
failure_condition: Both conditions triggered but 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in any 2-year rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales RSAFS decline month-over-month by more than 0.3% while housing starts HOUST simultaneously fall, indicating synchronized demand destruction across consumer and housing sectors that predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as forward earnings are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month decline > 0.3% AND HOUST month-over-month decline > 5%
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 718,867 million dollars in April 2010
regime_hint: demand-side contraction with synchronized weakness across consumer and housing
failure_condition: Both conditions triggered but 60-day forward returns exceed +2% in more than 60% of instances, or the interaction effect shows Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period, signaling labor market deterioration and forward-looking consumer spending weakness that produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as equity risk premiums expand.
failure_condition: UNRATE rises more than 0.3pp over 3 months but S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate FEDFUNDS declines below 3.0% while CPI month-over-month growth remains positive, signaling monetary accommodation with controlled inflation that reduces equity discount rates and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary easing with stable inflation expectations
failure_condition: Both conditions triggered but 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in any 2-year rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production INDPRO declines month-over-month by more than 0.8% while payroll employment PAYEMS simultaneously contracts, indicating synchronized supply and labor-side weakness that signals recession risk and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprice tail risk.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month decline > 0.8% AND PAYEMS month-over-month decline > 50 thousand persons
failure_condition: Both conditions triggered but 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances, or the interaction term shows Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 18-month window.
